cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cel: noun. A piece of celluloid on which has been drawn a frame of an animated film.
celery: noun. A European herb (Apium graveolens) of the carrot family.
cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.
center: noun. The point in the interior of a circle that is equidistant from all points on the circumference.
centre: noun. Australia, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, and South Africa standard spelling of center.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
creel: noun. A woven basket, especially a wicker basket.
crenel: noun. The space between merlons in a crenelated battlement.
cry: verb. To shed tears; to weep, especially in anger or sadness.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eely: adjective. Resembling an eel: long, thin and slippery.
elect: noun. One chosen or set apart.
enter: verb. To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.
erect: adjective. Upright; vertical or reaching broadly upwards.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
eye: noun. An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
eyer: noun. One who eyes someone or something.
eyne: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
eyre: noun. A journey taken by certain Medieval English itinerant judges (justices in eyre)
lectern: noun. A stand with a slanted top used to support a Bible from which passages are read during a church service.
lee: noun. A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.
leer: verb. To look sideways or obliquely; now especially with sexual desire or malicious intent.
leery: adjective. Cautious, suspicious, wary, hesitant, or nervous about something; having reservations or concerns.
leet: noun. A portion or list, especially a list of candidates for an office; also the candidates themselves.
lent: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Lent.
let: verb. To allow to, not to prevent.
ley: noun. Arable land used temporarily for hay or grazing.
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
lyre: noun. An ancient stringed musical instrument (a yoke lute chordophone) of Greek origin, consisting of two arms extending from a body to a
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
net: noun. A mesh of string, cord or rope.
recent: adjective. Having happened a short while ago.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
reel: noun. A shaky or unsteady gait.
relent: noun. A stay; a stop; a delay.
relet: verb. To let a property again
rely: verb. To trust; to have confidence in; to depend.
rent: noun. A payment made by a tenant at intervals in order to lease a property.
rente: noun. In France, interest payable by government on indebtedness; the bonds, shares, stocks, etc. that represent government indebtedness.
ret: verb. To prepare (flax, hemp etc.) for further processing by soaking, which facilitates separation of fibers from the woody parts of the
rete: noun. A network of blood vessels or nerves.
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
tee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter T / t.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
tel: noun. Alternative form of tell (“hill or mound”).
tele: noun. Alternative spelling of telly (“television, telegraph, telephone, teleport, telecommunication”).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tenrec: noun. Any of several diverse small mammals, of the family Tenrecidae, many native to Madagascar.
terce: noun. The third hour of daylight (about 9 am).
tern: noun. Any of various seabirds of the subfamily Sterninae (of the family Laridae) that are similar to gulls but are smaller and have a
tree: noun. A perennial woody plant taller and larger than a shrub with a wooden trunk and, at some distance from the ground, having leaves and
trey: noun. A playing card or die with the rank of three.
tyee: noun. A tribal chief of the Chinookan peoples in the Pacific Northwest of the United States.
tyer: noun. Dated form of tier (“a person or device that ties”).
tyre: noun. The ring-shaped protective covering around a wheel which is usually made of rubber or plastic composite and is either pneumatic or
yen: noun. The unit of Japanese currency (symbol: ¥) since 1871, divided into 100 sen.
yet: adverb. Thus far; up to the present; up to some unspecified time.
ceryl: noun. A radical, C 27 H 55, supposed to exist in several compounds obtained from waxes.
cetyl: noun. The univalent hexadecyl radical C 16 H 33 - present in many waxes.
clyer: noun. A swelling of the lymph node of cattle
creen: verb. To complain; to whine; to moan.
crl: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern East Cree.
crt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iyojwa'ja Chorote.
ctene: noun. A band of fused cilia on the bodies of ctenophores, used for locomotion.
cyte: noun. Synonym of cell (“quantity of protoplasm, containing a nucleus, enclosed within a cell membrane”).
elne: verb. To strengthen; hearten; comfort; encourage.
elt: verb. To injure (anything) by rough handling; handle roughly.
ert: verb. To incite; urge on; encourage.
eyn: noun. Alternative form of eyen: plural of eye.
neet: noun. Alternative letter-case form of NEET.
nete: noun. In Ancient Greek musical theory, the highest-pitched fixed note in the farther tetrachord on a lyre, always pitched a perfect fourth
nye: noun. A brood or flock of pheasants.
nyet: noun. A no; a negative response (in a Russian context).
reen: noun. A running waterway that links a ditch or stream to a river.
rynt: verb. stand off; move away (said by milkmaids to their cows after milking them)
teer: verb. To stir, as a calico - printer 's sieve.
telyn: noun. A type of Celtic harp.
tln: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Talondo'.
tlr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Talise.
yee: pronoun. You (the people being addressed); alternative spelling of ye.
yelt: noun. A young sow who has not yet had a litter.
yere: pronoun. your (plural); of ye, belonging to ye
yern: adverb. eagerly, heartily, gladly, willingly, earnestly.
